articles.title: No relief from prevailing heat and humidity for next three days in Odisha: Manorama Mohanty - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
People across Odisha are unlikely to get relief from the prevailing hot and humid weather conditions over the next three days, the Regional Meteorological Centre, Bhubaneswar, informed on Thursday.
The weather office has forecast intense heat and humidity across several parts of the state, making conditions uncomfortable for residents.
The Meteorological Department has also issued a warning for Nor'wester (thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds) for the next five days. In view of the changing weather conditions, alerts have been issued for Cuttack and Bhubaneswar for rainfall and strong winds.
For the next 24 hours, the weather department has issued a heatwave warning for Sambalpur district, urging people to take necessary precautions against extreme temperatures.
According to the Meteorological Department, eight locations in Odisha recorded temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius during the past 24 hours. Boudh emerged as the hottest place in the state, recording a maximum temperature of 42.7 degrees Celsius.
Meanwhile, rainfall was recorded in some parts of the state. Daringbadi recorded the highest rainfall, said Manorama Mohanty, Director of the Regional Meteorological Centre.

The weather office has advised people to remain cautious as the state is expected to witness a combination of scorching temperatures, high humidity, and thunderstorm activities over the coming days.
"Boudh has recorded a maximum temperature of 42.7 degrees Celsius in the last 24 hours. Kandhamal has experienced rainfall of 58 mm during the period. Many parts of the state will experience hot and humid weather conditions over the next three days. Sambalpur will witness heatwave conditions in the next 24 hours. Some parts of the state will experience light to moderate rainfall," said Manorama Mohanty, Director of the Regional Meteorological Centre, Bhubaneswar.
